title: Hyper-V VM Placement and Live Migration Failure Rate Detection
exclusions: Standalone Hyper-V hosts (single-node, no failover-cluster) — no Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole flow; VMs explicitly marked non-migratable due to GPU passthrough (DDA) or RemoteFX vGPU; VMware vSphere vMotion (different hypervisor); VirtualBox / QEMU live-migration on Linux (not Hyper-V); VMs migrated through SCVMM Workflow rather than direct Move-VM (separate audit trail).
dataSources: `index=hyperv` (mirrors such as `wineventlog`, `windows`, `hci_cluster`, `azure_stack_hci` per naming convention) blending Universal Forwarder **WinEventLog** inputs ingested via **`Splunk_TA_windows`** (**Splunkbase 742**) — **`Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Operational`** (**22106** live migration started, **22112** completed, **22113** failed, **22114** cancelled, **22117** storage live migration completed, **22125** saved-state migrated, **21024** destination start fault post-move, **21036** VHDX lock fault during storage migration, **25030** clustered VM brought online elsewhere post-failover), **`Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Networking`** for virtual-switch pressure during migration paths, **`Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Worker-Operational`** for worker faults, **`Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational`** (**1069** resource fault, **1146** RHS fault against **`vmclusres.dll`** backed roles, **1244** failover failure, **5115** CSV manual remount, **5142** CSV offline). **`Perfmon://Hyper-V VM Migration`** counters (**Migration Bytes Sent**, **Migration Bytes Received**, **Send-Receive Pending Bytes**) via TA perfmon. Scripted **HEC** lines for **`hyperv:vm`** (`Get-VM` JSON every ~5 minutes) and **`hyperv:cluster:affinity`** where each event carries **`Get-ClusterAffinityRule`** fields such as **`RuleType`** (**`SameNode`** / **`DifferentNode`**), optional **`-Soft`** soft/hard semantics, plus **`Get-ClusterOwnerNode`** / **`Set-ClusterOwnerNode`** possible-owner snapshots for placement enforcement review.
app: **`Splunk_TA_windows`** ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) on every Hyper-V cluster node or an aggregating Heavy Forwarder; pair with **Universal Forwarder** for `WinEventLog` and **`perfmon`** stanzas. Custom app or **HEC** receiver for scripted affinity and `Get-VM` JSON (sourcetypes **`hyperv:cluster:affinity`** and **`hyperv:vm`**). Optional Git-stored wrappers that call **`Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole`**, **`Get-ClusterAffinityRule`**, **`Set-ClusterAffinityRule`**, **`Get-ClusterPossibleOwner`**, **`Set-ClusterOwnerNode`**, **`Set-ClusterPreferredOwner`**, and **`Get-ClusterLog`** for runbook extraction (not required for baseline detection).
spl:
(index=hyperv OR index=wineventlog OR index=windows OR index=hci_cluster OR index=azure_stack_hci)
(sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Operational" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Networking" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Worker-Operational" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational" OR sourcetype="hyperv:vm" OR sourcetype="hyperv:cluster:affinity" OR sourcetype="Perfmon:Hyper-V VM Migration")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventCode,event_id,EventID,""))
| eval vmms_state=case(evt IN ("22106"),"lm_start",evt IN ("22112"),"lm_ok",evt IN ("22113"),"lm_fail",evt IN ("22114"),"lm_cancel",evt IN ("22117"),"slm_done",evt IN ("22125"),"quick_save_move",evt IN ("21024"),"dest_start_fail",evt IN ("21036"),"vhdx_lock",evt IN ("25030"),"postfailover_online",1=1,null())
| eval cluster_fault=case(evt IN ("1069","1244"),"resource_or_failover_fault",evt IN ("1146"),"rhs_crash",evt IN ("5115","5142"),"csv_stress",1=1,null())
| eval ruletype=upper(trim(coalesce(RuleType,rule_type,AffinityRuleType,"")))
| eval soft_flag=trim(tostring(coalesce(Soft,soft,isSoft,"")))
| eval mig_bytes_sent='Migration Bytes Sent'
| eval transport_stress=if(sourcetype="Perfmon:Hyper-V VM Migration" AND isnum(mig_bytes_sent) AND mig_bytes_sent>5000000000,1,0)
| eval rdma_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(smbdirect|rdma|network.?direct)"),1,0)
| where isnotnull(vmms_state) OR isnotnull(cluster_fault) OR sourcetype="hyperv:cluster:affinity" OR transport_stress=1 OR rdma_hint=1
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name,cluster_name,FQDN_Cluster,"")))
| stats count as seq_count values(evt) as codes values(vmms_state) as vmms_mix values(cluster_fault) as cl_faults latest(ruletype) as ruletype latest(soft_flag) as soft_flag max(transport_stress) as hot_perf max(rdma_hint) as rdma_hint dc(host) as nodes_seen by cluster_key host
| eval placement_hot=if(mvfind(vmms_mix,"lm_fail")>=0 OR mvfind(vmms_mix,"lm_cancel")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"rhs_crash")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"resource_or_failover_fault")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"csv_stress")>=0,1,0)
| eval posture=case(placement_hot=1,"placement_mobility_hot",hot_perf=1 OR seq_count>40,"saturation_or_noise",rdma_hint=1,"rdma_transport_review",1=1,"calm")
| where posture!="calm"
| sort -seq_count
| table cluster_key host seq_count codes vmms_mix cl_faults ruletype soft_flag hot_perf rdma_hint nodes_seen posture
description: Trends **Hyper-V** guest **mobility** failures and noisy move attempts across **Failover Cluster** nodes—live-migration lifecycle signals (**22106**/**22112**/**22113**/**22114**) plus storage-move completions (**22117**) and brittle paths (**21036**/**21024**/**25030**), **RHS** faults (**1146**) touching **`vmclusres.dll`** virtual-machine cluster resources, CSV strain (**5115**/**5142**) concurrent with migration waves, **SMB Direct**/**RDMA** migration-transport stalls surfaced from **`Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Networking`** combined with **`Perfmon:Hyper-V VM Migration`** saturation, and periodic **`hyperv:cluster:affinity`** JSON describing **`DifferentNode`**/**`SameNode`** **`-Soft`** posture versus **`Set-ClusterOwnerNode`**/**`Set-ClusterPreferredOwner`** reality.
value: Guests that cannot glide during **Invoke-CauRun** drains or **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** waves overload surviving blades and postpone patching—often silently until latency spills outward. Watching **`Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole`**-initiated migrations beside **`Move-VM`** telemetry exposes placement-rule collisions (**`Set-ClusterAffinityRule`** hard **`DifferentNode`** pairs versus pinned **`Owners`** lists), captures **Replica** drills mistaken for outages when **`22113`** bursts align with **`Start-VMFailover`** rehearsals, and separates **SMB** fabric contention from genuine **Hyper-V** scheduler faults before **`MaximumVirtualMachineMigrations`** concurrency masks saturating NIC pairs.
implementation: Deploy **`Splunk_TA_windows`** WinEventLog stanzas for **VMMS**, **Worker**, **FailoverClustering**, plus **`Perfmon://Hyper-V VM Migration`** on each node into **`hyperv`**. Publish **`hyperv:vm`** five-minute **`Get-VM`** snapshots and nightly **`hyperv:cluster:affinity`** **`Get-ClusterAffinityRule`** exports via **HEC**. Schedule the **`spl`** saved search with suppression keyed to **`cluster_key`** plus **`invoke_cau`**/**`chs_maintenance`** lookup tags.
visualization: (1) Column chart: seq_count by posture over trailing 24h. (2) Timeline: **22106**→**22113** streaks per host with rdma_hint overlay. (3) Table: cluster_key, host, vmms_mix, cl_faults, ruletype, soft_flag, nodes_seen. (4) Heatmap: Perfmon Migration Bytes Received tertiles versus lm_fail density. (5) Single-value lm_ok versus lm_fail ratio across rolling 7d slices.
knownFalsePositives: **Cluster-aware-update drain choreography** — **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** plus **`Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole`** deliberately stacks **`22106`**→**`22112`** bursts across minutes while **`MaximumVirtualMachineMigrations`** stays elevated; mute alerts when **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups tie **`invoke_cau`** job identifiers to those hosts. **Replica rehearsal mornings** — **`Start-VMFailover`** test steps alongside **`Resume-VMReplication`** churn mimic **`22113`** spikes absent structural faults because **`22114`** cancellations dominate scripted rollback drills—cross-check **`Hyper-V Replica`** planner logs before blaming **`SMB Direct`**. **GPU Discrete Device Assignment guests** remain pinned—**`22113`** pairs with **`lm_cancel`** simply prove **`DifferentNode`** desires conflict with hard **DDA** wiring; treat as informational unless destination **R2** removes **DDA** first. **RemoteFX-era vGPU assignments** still block online moves on older guest OS images; expect repeated **`22113`** strings until guests power down—suppress via **`hardware_profile_remotevx`** CMDB markers. **AVMA tenant mismatch** across stretched racks triggers **`22113`** when destination refuses activation—looks identical to networking failures except **`21024`** clusters near KMS chatter—route licensing SMEs instead of fabric rotations. **Compression-enabled migration waves** intentionally CPU-bound trade **`22112`** latency for narrower **`Perfmon`** **`pending_bytes`**—adjust thresholds upward during **`Set-VMHost`** experimental **`Compression`** pilots documented by infrastructure RFC numbers.
hardware: Hyper-V cluster nodes — Dell PowerEdge R750/R760, HPE ProLiant DL380, Lenovo SR650, Cisco UCS C240, Supermicro 2029U with optional RDMA NICs (Mellanox ConnectX-5/6/7, Chelsio T6, Marvell FastLinQ) for SMB Direct live-migration transport. Compatible across Hyper-V running on Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 Datacenter and Azure Stack HCI 23H2.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **Splunk Universal Forwarder** plus **Splunk_TA_windows** (Splunkbase 742) on each Hyper-V cluster member participating in **Move-VM** / **Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole** workflows—same build across nodes so props/transform parity preserves EventCode extraction.
• Dedicated service principals for scripted **Get-ClusterAffinityRule** collectors—avoid interactive **Domain Admin** shells; rotate **HEC** secrets quarterly alongside **Set-ClusterOwnerNode** freezes tied to CAB approvals.
• Migration subnets carrying **SMB** multi-channel flows (TCP plus optional **RDMA**) toward Mellanox/Chelsio/Marvell adapters described under hardware—inline IDS appliances that inject RST frames can spike **22113** until passive taps exempt migration VLANs.
• Primary index hyperv with mirrored wineventlog during brownfield onboarding—macros must union both names so SOC clipboard snippets stay stable between regions.
• Lookup **hyperv_migration_suppress** maintains **cluster_key**, window_id, invoke_cau_job so CAU drains never masquerade as surprise mobility regressions during paging bridges.

Step 1 — Configure **WinEventLog**, perfmon, and scripted JSON lanes
(1) On each forwarder local inputs.conf enable **WinEventLog**://**Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Operational**, **WinEventLog**://**Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Networking**, **WinEventLog**://**Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Worker-Operational**, and **WinEventLog**://**Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational** targeting index = hyperv with checkpointInterval tuned high enough that bursts of **22113** never lag beyond operator tolerance (inspect **splunkd.log** **TailReader** warnings before tightening).
(2) Create perfmon://Hyper-V VM Migration referencing **Migration Bytes Sent**, **Migration Bytes Received**, and **Send-Receive Pending Bytes** so **saturation_or_noise** posture can distinguish **NIC** saturation from transient **CSV** chatter before **Set-VMHost -MaximumVirtualMachineMigration** overrides ride through CAB review.
(3) Schedule a lightweight PowerShell wrapper every 5 minutes executing Get-VM | Select-Object Name,State,Status,ComputerName,Uptime | **ConvertTo-Json**, posting to sourcetype hyperv:vm via **HEC**—this inventory stream highlights **22125** saved-state migrations that should be rare beside **22112** successes.
(4) Hourly (or 15 minutes when affinity churn is high) emit **Get-ClusterAffinityRule** plus **Get-ClusterOwnerNode** snapshots to sourcetype **hyperv:cluster:affinity**, persisting RuleType SameNode versus **DifferentNode** plus soft/hard semantics from **Set-ClusterAffinityRule** so operators can compare Splunk rows to live **Failover Cluster Manager** **affinity panes** without RDP hopping.
(5) Document that **Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole** is the **FailoverClusters** orchestration verb while **Move-VM** is the Hyper-V module primitive—label dashboard drilldowns accordingly so RCA notes match the cmdlet your runbook cites.

Step 2 — Publish searches, macros, and alert semantics
Fold the index and sourcetype union into macros/**hyperv_migration_base** so Wave-2 teams can prepend a single backtick macro without retyping seventeen OR clauses. Keep coalesce(EventCode,event_id,EventID,...) in one macro named **hyperv_evt_code** because **Splunk_TA_windows** field casing drifts across Windows build levels.

Paste the SPL from the spl field into Search → Save As → Alert only after validating a 15-minute smoke window with head 200 to ensure **cl_faults** multi-value fields populate when **1146** fires.

```spl
(index=hyperv OR index=wineventlog OR index=windows OR index=hci_cluster OR index=azure_stack_hci)
(sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Operational" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Networking" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Worker-Operational" OR sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational" OR sourcetype="hyperv:vm" OR sourcetype="hyperv:cluster:affinity" OR sourcetype="Perfmon:Hyper-V VM Migration")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventCode,event_id,EventID,""))
| eval vmms_state=case(evt IN ("22106"),"lm_start",evt IN ("22112"),"lm_ok",evt IN ("22113"),"lm_fail",evt IN ("22114"),"lm_cancel",evt IN ("22117"),"slm_done",evt IN ("22125"),"quick_save_move",evt IN ("21024"),"dest_start_fail",evt IN ("21036"),"vhdx_lock",evt IN ("25030"),"postfailover_online",1=1,null())
| eval cluster_fault=case(evt IN ("1069","1244"),"resource_or_failover_fault",evt IN ("1146"),"rhs_crash",evt IN ("5115","5142"),"csv_stress",1=1,null())
| eval ruletype=upper(trim(coalesce(RuleType,rule_type,AffinityRuleType,"")))
| eval soft_flag=trim(tostring(coalesce(Soft,soft,isSoft,"")))
| eval mig_bytes_sent='Migration Bytes Sent'
| eval transport_stress=if(sourcetype="Perfmon:Hyper-V VM Migration" AND isnum(mig_bytes_sent) AND mig_bytes_sent>5000000000,1,0)
| eval rdma_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(smbdirect|rdma|network.?direct)"),1,0)
| where isnotnull(vmms_state) OR isnotnull(cluster_fault) OR sourcetype="hyperv:cluster:affinity" OR transport_stress=1 OR rdma_hint=1
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name,cluster_name,FQDN_Cluster,"")))
| stats count as seq_count values(evt) as codes values(vmms_state) as vmms_mix values(cluster_fault) as cl_faults latest(ruletype) as ruletype latest(soft_flag) as soft_flag max(transport_stress) as hot_perf max(rdma_hint) as rdma_hint dc(host) as nodes_seen by cluster_key host
| eval placement_hot=if(mvfind(vmms_mix,"lm_fail")>=0 OR mvfind(vmms_mix,"lm_cancel")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"rhs_crash")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"resource_or_failover_fault")>=0 OR mvfind(cl_faults,"csv_stress")>=0,1,0)
| eval posture=case(placement_hot=1,"placement_mobility_hot",hot_perf=1 OR seq_count>40,"saturation_or_noise",rdma_hint=1,"rdma_transport_review",1=1,"calm")
| where posture!="calm"
| sort -seq_count
| table cluster_key host seq_count codes vmms_mix cl_faults ruletype soft_flag hot_perf rdma_hint nodes_seen posture
```

Field rationale: vmms_state maps **Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VMMS-Operational** lifecycle IDs (**22106**/**22112**/**22113**/**22114**) onto short tokens; cluster_fault captures FailoverClustering signals such as **1146** **RHS** crashes involving **vmclusres.dll**-hosted resources; **hyperv:cluster:affinity** rows may lack evt yet still satisfy the opening where clause so RuleType enrichment survives for placement audits.

Macro tip: optional macro **hyperv_hec_optional** gates hyperv:vm sourcetype inclusion for lab clusters that only stream **WinEventLog** during pilot phases.

Step 3 — Compare Splunk rows to interactive Windows tools
(1) Event Viewer → Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → Hyper-V-VMMS → Operational filtered to **22113** must show the same status or subcomponent codes as indexed Message text—screenshot pairing is your change-record evidence pack.
(2) **Get-ClusterResource** -Name "Virtual Machine *" combined with **SeparateMonitor** probes explains whether **RHS** runs in isolated processes per VM when **1146** repeats—attach outputs to CMDB service records.
(3) **Test-VMReplicationConnection** should run outside **Invoke-CauRun** hours so Replica validation traffic is not misread as mobility failure spikes inside the same dashboards.
(4) Compare live **Get-ClusterAffinityRule** output against JSON lines: watch for stale **ConvertTo-Json** depth truncating nested VMList collections that still matter to **DifferentNode** enforcement.

Step 4 — Operationalise handoffs and dashboards
(1) Route **placement_mobility_hot** posture to virtualization on-call with **cluster_key** extracted from ComputerName macros so **Failover Cluster Manager** scope matches Splunk filters.
(2) Ticket template should reference **Get-ClusterLog** -Destination bundles whenever **1146** and **22113** overlap in a five-minute bucket—**RHS** memory dumps beside **vmclusres.dll** help Microsoft support timelines.
(3) Export weekly **CSV** snapshots of posture by cluster to pair with documented **Set-VMHost -MaximumVirtualMachineMigrations** values so **NIC** refresh projects can anticipate **saturation_or_noise** spikes.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes
• mvfind returns -1 unexpectedly after TA refresh—re-run fieldsummary on **WinEventLog** subset, extend coalesce lists, and reload **Splunk_TA_windows** fields.conf on search heads before rewriting eval logic.
• **cluster_key** blank on pure perfmon rows—join perfmon hosts to hyperv:vm ComputerName via lookup enrichment so stats grouping does not fracture unknown_* placeholders.
• **rdma_transport_review** noise during **NIC** firmware flashes—Mellanox mlxfwmanager resets BAR mappings; suppress rows via CMDB firmware_wave tags captured in **hyperv_migration_suppress**.
• Cluster of **22114** cancellations after accidental **Set-ClusterOwnerNode** edits—compare Affinity JSON Owners arrays against **Failover Cluster Manager** GUI lists before blaming **SMB Direct** fabrics.
• Cross-cluster Live Migration (Windows Server 2019 onward) splits telemetry across two **cluster_key** names unless partner_cluster enrichment arrives via scripted inputs—extend JSON feeds accordingly.
• Replica secondary queues replay **22125** saves during backlog drains—tie-break using **Hyper-V Replica** role counters before paging hypervisor SMEs about mobility faults.
• QoS policies steering Migration traffic onto unintended **SMB** interfaces inflate pending_bytes counters—pair **saturation_or_noise** posture with **Get-SmbMultichannelConnection** reviews before **NIC** swaps.
• Simultaneous **5115**, **5142**, and **22113** clusters typically indict **CSV**/storage planes rather than **Move-VM** scheduling—route evidence to Storage bridges before NetOps escalation paths open.
• **RHS** LooksAlive versus IsAlive skew on massive memory VMs—evaluate **SeparateMonitor** adoption trade-offs documented alongside **vmclusres.dll** isolation guidance when **1146** repeats despite healthy **CSV** stacks.
• Compression pilots via Set-VMHost trade CPU overhead for narrower queues—tag migration_mode=compress hosts inside lookups so saturation thresholds stay aligned with experimentation RFC IDs.
• Duplicate affinity JSON shipments after scheduler retries—hash JSON payloads per minute bucket inside **HEC** preprocessor scripts so Splunk never double-counts identical RuleType rows during transient **WinRM** noise.
grandmaExplanation: We watch guests sliding between Hyper-V hosts during drain sweeps or ordinary rebalances. When moves stutter, cancel, or clash with placement fences, we point it out early before workloads bunch onto one strained blade.
